rules.title: Temporal: Workflow Task StartToClose Timeout Detected

annotations.summary: Temporal: workflow task StartToClose timeout in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} ({{ $value | printf "%.4f" }} timeouts/s)
annotations.description:
Workflow task StartToClose timeout detected in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}. The server received no workflow task completion or failure response within the workflowTaskTimeout deadline (default 10s).

For workflow tasks running local activities, this can indicate:
- Total results of all local activities within the workflow task exceeded the default gRPC message size limit (4MB), preventing the response from being sent.
- The workflow task heartbeat sent by the SDK at 80% of the WFT timeout was not received by the server, or was throttled, before the timeout fired.

In both cases, local activities will run again on workflow task retry, potentially on a different worker.

rules.title: Temporal: Workflow Task Heartbeat Timeout Detected

annotations.summary: Temporal: workflow task heartbeat timeout in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} ({{ $value | printf "%.4f" }} timeouts/s)
annotations.description:
Workflow task heartbeat timeout detected in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}. This fires when local activity or activities, including all their retries, ran for more than 30 minutes without completing or failing.

Local activities should always have a ScheduleToClose timeout set to less than 30 minutes — the WorkflowTaskHeartbeatTimeout is the absolute ceiling.

rules.title: Temporal: Workflow Task ScheduleToStart Timeout Detected

annotations.summary: Temporal: workflow task ScheduleToStart timeout in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} ({{ $value | printf "%.4f" }} timeouts/s)
annotations.description:
Workflow task ScheduleToStart timeout detected in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }}. A workflow task was scheduled on the sticky task queue but was not picked up by the owning worker within the sticky schedule-to-start timeout (default 5s, configured by the SDK via StickyScheduleToStartTimeout).

This timeout only applies to workflow tasks on the sticky task queue — normal task queue workflow tasks do not have a ScheduleToStart timeout. When this fires, the workflow task is rescheduled on the normal task queue.
